channel.title: 2026-06-21 AI × Science 文献日报 RSS
channel.description: 2026-06-21 当天筛选出的 AI × 物理 / 化学 / 材料交叉文献 RSS。

item.title: A magnetic environment with reproducible spatio-temporal magnetic conditions at picotesla level
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Philipp Wunderl, Maximilian Huber, Florian Büttiker, Jonas Emrich, Peter Ewert, Peter Fierlinger 等19位作者
Walk-in, picotesla-scale environments are essential for measurements of biomagnetism and fundamental physics. However, conventional rooms require typical active dynamic compensation, which adds complexity and magnetic noise. Here, we present a solution that achieves an absolute residual field well below 100pT within its central measurement volume. Following magnetic equilibration, this environment achieves picotesla-scale reproducibility. Consequently, optically pumped magnetometers operate at their design noise and drift performance and remain operational during sensor motion without active feedback. A key technique is robotic mapping, which resolves the ultra-low residual field patterns and demagnetization stability. We demonstrate the platform's versatility through high-fidelity adult…

item.title: A Local Ontological Model of Double-Slit Fringes via Moiré Sampling and Non-Linear Detection
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 BAYKAL SARIOGLU
Feynman’s classic exposition of the double-slit experiment asserts that no local, particle-only ontology can reproduce quantum interference fringes without invoking wave-particle duality or nonlocal communication. We re-examine the implicit assumptions of this paradigm by constructing a strictly local, counterfactual-definite sampling model. The framework relies on four components: (i) an oscillating detector substrate that modulates single-particle detection efficiency; (ii) a geometric moir´e (beat) pattern emerging from independent slit trajectories; (iii) a finite detector relaxation memory that provides a non-linear readout; and (iv) source-detector phase coherence. We demonstrate that while linear averaging over the sub-wavelength carrier landscape washes out the signal (the “Additi…

item.title: Quantum gravity research links continuous parameters to local operators within the theory itself
item.description:
📚 Physics News
A researcher at Kyushu University and his collaborators have shown that continuous parameters in quantum gravity may not be freely adjustable "dials" from outside the theory, but rather arise from operators within the theory itself, supporting the century-old claim by Albert Einstein about the fundamental laws of nature.
